body.title: Register pe_* per-step functions as MCPCALL tools
body.result: 12 per-step pe_* functions registered as MCPCALL tools; registration tests added
body.description: ENGRAM_CODE_* payloads MCPCALL the per-step pe_* functions (pe_entry_init, pe_claim, pe_read_ticket, pe_plan, pe_filter, pe_situate, pe_observe, pe_hypothesize, pe_implement, pe_test, pe_probe, pe_close_loop). These functions exist in pe_chain.py but are NOT registered as tools via registry.register — only the high-level wrappers (run_pe_chain, run_pe_plan, run_pe_filter, run_pe_probe) are registered (pe_chain.py:2076-2145). So even after T-engram-trigger-cell-name-mismatch lands, MCPCALL pe_entry_init hits the unknown-tool branch (node_executor.py:387-396) silently. Fix: add registry.register calls for each of the 12 per-step pe_* functions. Each registration needs name, description (one-liner from function docstring), parameters schema (derive from signature), and fn lambda. Scope: pe_chain.py registrations only. Does not change per-step function signatures. Does not touch node_executor. Test plan: after registration, run a dry MCPCALL on each of the 12 steps, assert no 'unknown tool' warnings. Then T-engram-trigger-cell-name-mismatch's integration test can validate the full chain end-to-end.
